title: Japan develops a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries
titleZh: 日本开发出从废旧电动汽车电池中回收高达90%锂的新方法
author: donohoe
relevanceReason: 该帖子报道了日本科学家开发出一种从废旧电动汽车电池中回收高达90%锂的新方法，属于科技领域的环保与新能源技术突破。
summaryRaw: In a ground-breaking step forward, Scientists from Japan have developed a new method to recover up to 90 percent of lithium from used EV batteries – and it suddenly feels like great news on Earth Day.
With electric vehicles booming worldwide, the pressure is mounting to find smarter ways to deal with old battery waste.
This new technique doesn’t just recycle materials; it recovers most of them at an unbelievable rate.
And if it delivers at scale, it could change how EV batteries are made and reused for years to come.
A new method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries
summaryZh: 日本科学家研发出从废旧电动汽车电池中回收高达90%锂的新方法。
该技术有望显著改善电动汽车电池废弃物的处理问题，并可能改变未来电池制造与再利用的方式。
highlightsZh: 回收率高达90%，远超传统回收方法。
针对全球电动汽车快速增长带来的电池废弃物问题提供了解决方案。
若实现规模化应用，将对电池制造和再利用行业产生深远影响。
在地球日之际发布，具有重要的环保意义。

comments.author: BaudouinVH
comments.textRawHtml:
The article is very, very light with details. The university or research center is not named. No scientist is named. No link. Nothing that tells "look, we're telling you real, solid, serious stuff."

Here is another article with that details : https://www.techspot.com/news/112051-japan-finds-way-recover...
comments.textZhHtml:
这篇文章的细节非常、非常匮乏。没有提及大学或研究中心的名称。没有提到任何科学家。没有链接。没有任何内容能让人感觉到“看，我们告诉你们的是真实、扎实、严肃的内容。”

这里还有另一篇包含这些细节的文章：https://www.techspot.com/news/112051-japan-finds-way-recover...

children.author: boutell
children.textRawHtml:
Thank you. Among other important points there:

"That said, Japan isn't the only country pursuing lithium recovery. In the US, Redwood Materials – the recycling company founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel – says it's already recovering 95% of lithium from the equivalent of about 250,000 EVs per year."

EV batteries are too large and valuable to wind up in landfills, although I'm sure it has happened somewhere. I do like that Japan is is making it harder to just throw out smaller lithium batteries. That strikes me as a more probable source of waste.
children.textZhHtml:
谢谢。其中还有其他几个重要观点：

“话虽如此，日本并不是唯一追求锂回收的国家。在美国，由特斯拉前首席技术官JB Straubel创立的回收公司Redwood Materials表示，它每年已经从相当于约25万辆电动汽车的电池中回收了95%的锂。”

电动汽车电池体积大、价值高，不太可能最终被扔进垃圾填埋场，尽管我敢肯定某个地方肯定发生过。我确实很欣赏日本让随意丢弃小型锂电池变得更难的做法。在我看来，这才是更有可能产生浪费的来源。

children.author: pixl97
children.textRawHtml: I would add that EV batteries are too flammable to end up in landfills. A few small batteries from things like phones and laptops generally make it through the waste processing chain without rupturing and causing problems, but a large pile of them outside of containment starts becoming a certainty that one will break and set the whole lot on fire.
children.textZhHtml: 我想补充一点，电动汽车电池的易燃性太高，不能直接扔进垃圾填埋场。像手机和笔记本电脑里的一些小电池通常能顺利通过废物处理流程而不会破裂引发问题，但如果是一大堆电池堆放在没有防护的地方，那几乎肯定会有电池破损并引发大火。

children.author: catigula
children.textZhHtml:
经典操作

>种族主义

>种族主义，日本

典型场景。

children.textRawHtml: I have no idea what you’re trying to say.

children.author: fragmede
children.textRawHtml: Despite the fact that they consider the linked website slop, it is appearing on HN's frontpage.

children.author: larodi
children.textRawHtml:
Because you not even trying

supercarblondie.com

Published article bout supposed breakthrough battery recovery with zero refs to actual sources as the other commenter noted.

So likely the article is LLM reprint of something else or aggregation. Thus blondie slop.

But even so it tops like crazy because … well because, in all fair evidence, HN folk seems to care zero about sources, or less so about authorship when is/gets excited about random shit, but in the same time cares badly whether sacred code in Linux kernel was AI written.

Now downvote more.
children.textZhHtml:
因为你根本没花心思

supercarblondie.com

发布了一篇关于所谓突破性电池回收的文章，正如另一位评论者指出的，完全没有引用任何实际来源。

所以这篇文章很可能是大语言模型对其他内容的转载或拼凑。因此这就是blondie的垃圾内容。

但即便如此，它还是火得一塌糊涂，因为……好吧，因为有充分的证据表明，HN的用户似乎根本不在乎信息来源，或者在对各种乱七八糟的东西感到兴奋时也不太在乎作者是谁，但同时又极其在意Linux内核里的神圣代码是不是AI写的。

现在再多点几个踩吧。

children.author: thebruce87m
children.textZhHtml: 这是人类制造的垃圾内容吗？

children.author: andrewclunn
children.textRawHtml: The "up to" seems to be doing a lot of work.

children.author: thegrim33
children.textRawHtml: Yup. Any time you see weasel words, especially this one, whether it's in advertisements, product descriptions, wherever, you can instantly dismiss the product/claim. It's such a quick and easy tell to notice.
children.textZhHtml: 没错。任何时候你看到那些含糊其辞的词语，尤其是这种词，无论是在广告、产品描述还是任何其他地方，你都可以立刻否定该产品或说法。这是一个非常快速且容易察觉的破绽。

children.author: neuroelectron
children.textRawHtml: Being able to quickly skim articles is definitely a skill that has paid in spades since the advent of AI slop.
children.textZhHtml: 自从AI生成的垃圾内容泛滥以来，能够快速略读文章绝对是一项让人受益匪浅的技能。

children.author: ufocia
children.textRawHtml: Still slop. This article appears to conflate battery recycling with lithium recovery, two distinct steps to achieve high lithium reuse.
children.textZhHtml: 依然是垃圾内容。这篇文章似乎把电池回收和锂回收混为一谈了，而这是实现高锂复用率的两个截然不同的步骤。

children.author: mrob
children.textRawHtml:
Doesn't read like slop to me. The only sentence that could be argued to conflate the two is:

>A new law taking effect this year will require manufacturers and importers to collect and recycle small portable batteries from the likes of phones, vapes, and power tools.

But "recycle" doesn't necessarily mean closed-loop recycling (recycling into the same products). Lithium recovery is a type of open-loop recycling and the surrounding paragraphs talk about lithium recovery so this is a reasonable interpretation.
children.textZhHtml:
在我看来这读起来不像是垃圾内容。唯一可能被指责混淆两者的句子是：

>今年生效的一项新法律将要求制造商和进口商回收手机、电子烟和电动工具等小型便携式电池。

但“回收”并不一定意味着闭环回收（回收成相同的产品）。锂回收是一种开环回收，而且上下文段落都在讨论锂回收，所以这是一个合理的解释。

comments.author: cyphar
comments.textRawHtml:
It really should not be surprising that we can get very high recovery percentages from batteries -- we do not mine elemental lithium, so the processes we use for extraction are already designed to extract lithium from fairly low-purity sources. In contrast, lithium batteries are an incredibly high-purity source of lithium. The main question is when it will become cost-effective to create recycling pipelines.

Lead acid batteries had a similar trajectory and modern lead acid batteries are effectively 100% recycled.
comments.textZhHtml:
我们能从电池中获得极高的回收率，这其实并不令人惊讶——我们并不开采单质锂，所以我们使用的提取工艺本来就是为从纯度相当低的来源中提取锂而设计的。相比之下，锂电池是纯度极高的锂来源。主要问题在于，何时建立回收流水线才能实现成本效益。

铅酸电池也经历了类似的发展轨迹，现代铅酸电池实际上实现了100%回收。

children.author: legitster
children.textRawHtml:
This is probably somewhat true, but also I suspect there might be an order of magnitude difference from extracting trace lithium from inert rock vs collecting it as a salt from amongst a medley of very refined metals.

Case in point - lead acid batteries are not a fair comparison. A lead acid battery is so robust you can separate the cathodes and anodes with your (gloved) hands. Getting the elements out of a lead battery is like picking pieces of pepperoni off of a pizza. Whereas taking lithium out of a lithium cell is like pulling only a certain protein out of a roll of bologna. And the protein catches fire in contact with air.
children.textZhHtml:
这可能有一定道理，但我也怀疑，从惰性岩石中提取微量锂与从一堆高度精炼的金属中将其作为盐类收集起来，两者之间可能存在一个数量级的差异。

举个例子——铅酸电池不是一个公平的比较对象。铅酸电池非常坚固，你甚至可以戴着手套用双手把阴极和阳极分开。从铅电池中提取元素就像从披萨上挑出意大利辣香肠片一样简单。而从锂电池中提取锂，则就像从一卷博洛尼亚香肠中只抽出某种特定的蛋白质。而且这种蛋白质一接触空气就会燃烧。
